critiqueProblems.title: Camera is broken inside the house — 'accepted as arcade' is untenable for the opening act
critiqueProblems.detail: Spring boom = 6.5r behind + 3.2r above (tuning.js CAM_DIST_K/CAM_HEIGHT_K). At house-exit sizes (r 0.4–0.7m) the boom is 2.6–4.6m in rooms only 4.6–4.8m wide with 2.4m closed-box walls and no ceiling. The camera height (1.3–2.2m) stays BELOW the wall tops (2.85m from h0.45 floor), so whenever the ball is within ~boom-length of a wall — i.e. most of the time in a 4.8m room — the camera is either embedded in a wall box (screen-filling solid) or outside the house with the ball fully occluded. This is the first 1–2 minutes of the game, the tutorial moment. It also corrupts steering: input is camera-relative (ballPhysics.js camYaw convention), so an occluded/popping camera breaks movement, not just the view. Secondary: fog far = 55r ≈ 2.75m at the 0.05m start (FOG_FAR_K), so the tatami room's far walls render as fog/sky color until r≈0.12m — the v1 fog law had nothing fixed-position to swallow; the house does. The v2 draft files this under risks as a Stream-C 'contingency'; it is actually a Phase-0 contract because it needs a Terrain query (wall raycast) that Stream A owns and Stream C consumes.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Promote to Phase-0/contract work: add Terrain.clampCameraBoom(ballPos, desiredCamPos) -> camPos (segment-vs-wall-AABB against the same static grid, shorten the boom to the nearest blocking wall minus margin, smooth the clamp with the existing critically-damped spring so yaw/steering doesn't snap). Pair it with an indoor boom factor (e.g. zone flag 'interior' in level.js zones scaling CAM_DIST/HEIGHT ~0.6x, crossfaded over 0.5s — static data, radius-continuous, seamlessness law intact). For fog, add FOG_FAR_MIN_M (~7m, real-meter floor converted at query time) so room walls are never fog-swallowed; verify it still satisfies the fog<loadRadius assert. Budget this in Stream A+C interfaces now, not as contingency.

critiqueProblems.title: One-absorb door soft-locks: GROWTH_K=10 makes the 1.2m/1.4m door gates a minefield, and the genkan one-way step strands small balls outside
critiqueProblems.detail: Shipped tuning is GROWTH_K=10 (not the design-doc 0.45): newR = cbrt(R^3 + 10*r^3). A 0.55m ball absorbing one 0.35m stool (legal: 0.35 <= 0.65*0.55) jumps to cbrt(0.166+0.429) = 0.84m in a single absorb — past BOTH the 1.2m interior doorway (needs r<0.6) and the 1.4m front door (r<0.7) with zero warning. The start (tatami) room's exit is the NARROWEST gate, so the most likely soft-lock is being sealed into the starting room with finite, never-respawning content and no shrink mechanic — a hard restart. Inverse trap: the front door is open from frame one; a player who rolls out at r<0.40 cannot climb the 0.40m genkan step back up (STEP_CLIMB_K=1.0), and the garden's band mask is {1,2} — there is nothing a sub-0.1m ball can absorb outside, so they are stranded forever. The draft's risk entry ('budget indoor content to ~0.5m') addresses average pacing, not these single-event cliffs.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Three concrete fixes: (1) invert door monotonicity — inner openings must be WIDER than outer ones (interior doorway >= 1.8m, genkan opening 1.8m, front door 1.4m as the single size gate), so growing too big only locks you OUT of inner rooms, never IN; (2) lower the genkan step to 0.10m (climbable at r>=0.10) and add a small band-0 sprinkle to the garden mask near the door so an early escapee can still grow; (3) telegraph the gate: when the ball bonks a doorway at diameter within 15% of the opening, fire the existing bounce/shake plus a HUD hint. Keep the 'vacuum the whole house' worst-case playthrough in Phase 3, but these structural changes are what remove the soft-locks.

critiqueProblems.title: Floor query semantics ('highest floor containing point') break the pond, under-table space, and the claimed radius-continuity
critiqueProblems.detail: Three concrete failures from one rule. (a) Pond F6 (h=-0.10, x[7.5,10] z[3.5,6]) lies INSIDE garden F5 (h=0): 'highest floor containing point' always returns F5, so the pond dip — and rare R3 placed in its corner — can never take effect. (b) Ball under a table: P1 top h0.80 over F1 h0.45; a ball with r<0.35 rolls under it, but groundSimY (unfiltered highest) returns 0.80 → the blob shadow renders ON TOP of the table above the ball, and knock-off restY/chunk-clutter y land on the tabletop instead of the floor. (c) Growth discontinuity: a ball sitting under the table that grows past r=0.35 has standSimY jump 0.45→0.80 instantly — the y-spring launches it up THROUGH the table slab. The design sells standSimY as 'continuous in radius = seamlessness law', but the climb gate makes stand height a step function of radius by construction; that is fine at walk-up edges, but fatal when the ball is already inside an elevated floor's footprint.
critiqueProblems.resolution: (1) Author floors strictly non-overlapping: carve F5 into a ring around F6 (4 rects), same for F7/F8. Add a level.js boot validator asserting no two floor rects overlap. (2) Model raised platforms (P1–P3, F11 pedestal) as SOLID PRISMS: the top floor rect plus implicit perimeter walls from the base floor to the top — the ball can then never occupy the footprint below the top, eliminating cases (b) and (c) entirely (this matches how the terrainMesh already renders them as boxes). (3) Give groundSimY a maxY/refY parameter ('highest floor with h <= refY + eps') for blob shadow and knock-off restY so they track the surface the ball is actually on.

critiqueProblems.title: House floor plan has a literal hole: x[0,4.6] z[-3.2,-1.2] has no floor — an h=0 pit inside the house; level data is elsewhere hand-waved
critiqueProblems.detail: Floors east of the interior wall are F2 kitchen z[0.2,3.2] and F3 hallway z[-1.2,0.2]; the region x[0,4.6] z[-3.2,-1.2] inside the house perimeter has no floor rect. groundSimY falls through to the h=0 base plane: a 0.45m drop off the hallway edge, the raw ground-shader grid visible indoors (no tatami/wood slab), and any ball with r<0.45 that rolls in cannot climb back out — another stranding pit, this one in the middle of the tutorial space. Separately, the garden enclosure is specified as 'back hedge x=-5.4 z[-7,7] + closers z=±6.5 x[-5.4,5.8]... (close the ring)' — an ellipsis in a binding data spec; the resulting wrap-around side yard behind the house is reachable from the garden (no wall at x=5.8 above z=0.6) and is a dead, zero-content space. Implementers will invent geometry here.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Author the missing room explicitly (e.g. F3b washitsu x[0,4.6] z[-3.2,-1.2] h0.45, opening to the hallway) or wall it off at z=-1.2. Fully enumerate the garden ring — no ellipses in level.js. Most importantly, make the Phase-0 level.js stub include a dev-mode validator: (a) every point inside a wall ring is covered by a floor; (b) every floor region is reachable from start under some radius in [0.05, 2.2] given the step/door rules; (c) opening widths and step heights printed as a gate table. This validator is cheap (the data is ~30 rects) and catches every authoring bug of this class before integration.

critiqueProblems.title: Two spawners share one ObjectStore with no ownership protocol — aliveCount cross-talk, sub-pixel sweep eats level placements, and rares can be permanently auto-consumed
critiqueProblems.detail: spawner.js _onAbsorb (line ~540) decrements _aliveCount and bumps _slotGen for EVERY absorb, including indices it never spawned — with LevelSpawner allocating ~260 placements from the same store, the chunk spawner's population gate (ALIVE_TOTAL_BUDGET check in _drainSpawn) drifts meaningless. Worse, _subPixelSweep scans ALL alive store slots and calls _despawnIndex(idx, fade, consume=true) — it will free LevelSpawner-owned slots behind its back (stale alive-rare ring, double-free on the level side) and, fatally for the rare hunt: golden candy R1 (diameter ~0.04m) crosses the sub-pixel threshold (0.04 * ballR) the moment the ball reaches ~1.0m — uncollected rares are silently destroyed mid-run, making rare 6/6 impossible and corrupting forEachAliveRare sparkles. The draft never specifies how the two spawners partition the store.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Define ownership in Phase 0: add FLAG_LEVEL (e.g. bit 16) set by LevelSpawner; chunk Spawner._onAbsorb, _subPixelSweep, and _despawnIndex skip flagged slots; LevelSpawner runs its own absorb bookkeeping and its own (much smaller) far-deactivate sweep. Additionally exempt FLAG_RARE objects from any size-based despawn — rares must persist until absorbed or run end (they are 6 objects; cost is nil). Add a dev assert that aliveCount(chunk) + aliveCount(level) == store alive total.

critiqueProblems.title: standSimY's curStand input is unspecified while pos.y is mid-spring — wall/climb classification races the underdamped y-spring
critiqueProblems.detail: terrain.collide and standSimY key blocking/climbing off 'curStandReal', but the draft never says where curStand comes from. If it is derived from ball.pos.y (the obvious implementation), it is wrong during every spring transient: rolling off the P2 TV stand (h0.85), pos.y decays over ~300ms (BALL_Y_OMEGA=16, ZETA=0.6 underdamped), during which the ball's apparent stand is still ~0.85 — wall tops and steps measured against it are briefly 0.4m 'shorter', letting the ball cross gates it shouldn't (and the reverse while popping upward after an absorb, walls briefly over-block). The underdamped spring also overshoots BELOW the target on the 0.40m genkan drop — the ball visibly dips under the floor plane and rubber-bounces, since vel.y is permanently 0 and there is no gravity model for drops.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Specify in types.js Phase 0: BallState.standSimY is authoritative TRACKED state, updated ONLY by terrain.collide from the accepted post-resolution XZ position (never read back from pos.y); all wall/step gates use it; the y-spring is pure cosmetics downstream of it. Clamp the spring output at standSimY + radiusSim*0.98 minimum so it can never render below the floor, and use the critically-damped variant (zeta>=1) when the target moves DOWN (drops) while keeping the underdamped pop for upward absorb growth. Put 'roll off platform near a wall' and 'absorb-pop next to a doorway' in Stream A's headless test matrix.

critiqueProblems.title: Knock-off flights ignore terrain entirely: objects fly through house walls and land at the wrong height on/off platforms
critiqueProblems.detail: spawner.reinject computes restY = r*(1+yOff) ONCE at launch (v2 adds groundSimY at the LAUNCH position), but _updateFlights integrates ballistic motion with no wall tests and lands at the precomputed restY. Indoors this means: bonk the kitchen counter, watch a mug sail through the shoji wall into the tatami room; get knocked off while ON the P1 table and the ejected object lands floating 0.35m above the floor (restY frozen at table height); knock-offs near the genkan drop land embedded in the lower floor. With v2's dash explicitly encouraging 'dash into walls bonks and knocks stuck objects off', this path is exercised constantly in the dense house zone. Also: LEVEL-spawned objects that get absorbed then knocked off re-enter via chunk-spawner reinject and lose FLAG_RARE/level identity (archetypeIndexFor only knows the 32 band ids) — what happens to raresFound when a counted rare is knocked back off is unspecified.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Per flight step (16 flights max, trivial cost): recompute restY from terrain.groundSimY(f.x, f.z) so landings always match the local floor; land immediately when terrain.collide-style wall test detects a crossing (or simply land on first wall contact — cosmetically fine). For rares: declare in the spec that rare credit is granted at first absorb and FLAG_RARE is stripped on knock-off (one sentence resolves the double-count question); assert spawner-table and objects.js code-table agreement for codes 0..31 since reinject and absorb now use different id tables.

critiqueProblems.title: Band mask is per-band, not per-archetype — the endgame 'cars as late meal' cannot coexist with band-3 houses on the same street zone
critiqueProblems.detail: The statue requires growing 1.4m→1.77m off band-3 content ('cars on the upper street are the intended late meal'), but band 3 is a single chunk band containing house/tree/car/truck/bus/kiosk/fountain/shed with weighted random picks. Any zone rect that enables band 3 over F8/the plaza floor (to get cars) also rolls houses and sheds there — multi-meter unclimbable pushback obstacles on a 9m-wide street, randomly blockading the path; restrict band 3 to the fenced scenery strips instead and no cars ever spawn where the ball can reach them, soft-locking the 1.4→1.77 leg exactly as risks fear. The chunk spawner has no per-archetype spatial filtering and adding one after the deterministic-draw marker would change which archetype lands where only by skipping — i.e. starving density unpredictably.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Do not use the chunk spawner for the late meal: author cars/trucks as curated LEVEL placements on F8 and the plaza fringe (LevelSpawner already handles arbitrary archetypes, deterministic positions, and the consumed array), sized so the 1.4→1.77 budget closes with ~40% surplus including the GROWTH_K=10 statue jump (absorbing the 1.15m statue takes a 1.77m ball to ~2.75m — also update the 'max ball ~2.2m' claim and re-verify the h>=3.0 bounds and the result-screen size copy against 2.75m). Keep band-3 chunk spawns strictly inside the fenced strips. Verify with ?at=plaza&r=1.4 as planned.

critiqueProblems.title: STEP_CLIMB_K applies to walls, so 1.8m street fences become climbable exactly at endgame sizes (r>=1.8m)
critiqueProblems.detail: The wall rule 'blocks iff (wallTopReal - standReal) > STEP_CLIMB_K * trueRadius' with K=1.0 means the h1.8 street fences and alcove-pocket fences stop blocking at r>=1.8m — which the ball reaches at the plaza endgame (statue absorb alone lands at ~2.75m, and the design itself ships cars absorbable at r>=1.7). The ball then rolls over the fence into the band-3 scenery strips (z[5,12]) among giant pushback houses, can get wedged between a house bounding sphere and the fence (pushback vs wall de-penetration fighting), and the curated alcove reward pockets lose their framing. Contained by the h3.0 outer ring, but it is an unintended traversal layer opening at precisely the moment the player should be funneled to the statue.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Either raise street/alcove fences to 2.8–3.0m (matching hedges/bounds — simplest, pure data), or add a per-wall boolean 'rigid' in level.js that opts a wall out of the climb rule. A static authored flag is not a radius/tier gate, so the seamlessness law is intact. Also add a Stream A headless test: ball at r just above a wall's climb threshold straddling the wall while absorb._pushback fires from an object on the far side — pushback runs AFTER terrain.collide in the substep and can shove the ball across a just-unblocked wall.

critiqueProblems.title: Phase 0 deletes WIN_RADIUS_M from tuning.js while scaleManager.js (Stream B) still imports it — the build breaks for all five streams
critiqueProblems.detail: fileChanges says tuning.js (Phase-0, lead-frozen) gets 'DELETE WIN_RADIUS_M', but src/world/scaleManager.js line 47 imports WIN_RADIUS_M and uses it in two places (grow progress exit, win latch), and scaleManager is Stream B work that lands LATER. The moment Phase 0 merges, every stream's dev build fails on the missing export — the 'zero file overlap' parallelization promise dies on day one. Same class of trap: tiers.js shrinking to 4 entries in Phase 0 while spawner.js sizes _cumW/_wTot from TIERS at module load (fine) but absorb.js sizes _collisionScale from objects.js ARCHETYPE_ID_BY_CODE — objects.js is Stream B too; the Phase-0 4-tier tiers.js plus the unmodified v1 objects.js assert path must be verified to boot.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Keep WIN_RADIUS_M exported (marked @deprecated) through Phase 2 and delete it in the integration pass, OR include the two-line scaleManager.js edit in Phase 0 explicitly (documented exception to zero-overlap, trivial merge risk). Add to the Phase-0 exit criteria: 'npm run dev boots with all Phase-0 contract files merged and zero stream files touched' — this catches every dangling-import trap of this kind mechanically.

critiqueProblems.title: BGM scheduler's 'interval thread' premise is wrong — it is main-thread allocation, a standing exception to the zero-alloc budget
critiqueProblems.detail: audio: 'allocation happens on the interval thread, never in the RAF loop' — JavaScript timers run on the same main thread as the RAF loop; a setInterval(25ms) callback creating <=10 oscillator/gain/noise nodes per beat at 132BPM is a continuous ~25 allocations/sec stream interleaved with frames, plus eventual GC. This contradicts the v1 budget '0 B/frame steady-state GC garbage' as literally stated. It is also standard, broadly acceptable WebAudio practice (v1 sfx.js already allocates nodes per event) — the problem is the false rationale, which will mislead the Stream D implementer into not measuring it.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Rewrite the rationale: declare an explicit, documented exemption to the zero-alloc law for WebAudio node creation (sfx + bgm), bounded at <=N nodes/sec, and add the BGM-on case to the Phase-3 iGPU profiling checklist (heap-delta line in the existing debug overlay already measures this). Cheap mitigations if it shows up: reuse persistent noise AudioBuffers and PeriodicWaves (the expensive allocations), keep per-tick scheduling under 0.2ms as specced.

critiqueProblems.title: ABSORB handler order is an undocumented contract: runStats.isRare validity races LevelSpawner's own ABSORB bookkeeping
critiqueProblems.detail: gameSystems specs isRare(objIndex) 'queried synchronously during the emit, before store.free reuse', but LevelSpawner ALSO subscribes ABSORB to mark its consumed array and free its placement bookkeeping. The v1 bus presumably calls handlers in subscription order; if main.js constructs LevelSpawner before RunStats (the natural construction order given the dependency arrow runStats(rareProvider=levelSpawner)), LevelSpawner may clear the very record isRare reads. Nothing in the draft pins handler order; v1 has the same pattern (render/ball reads store fields during the emit, spawner._onAbsorb mutates after) and survives only because its handlers touch disjoint state.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Cheapest fix: make isRare resilient instead of order-dependent — LevelSpawner keeps the FLAG_RARE store bit and its rare ring valid THROUGH its own ABSORB handler and defers cleanup to its next update() tick (one boolean). Alternatively, document in events.js (Phase-0 contract header) that for EVT.ABSORB the wiring order is: ball attach -> runStats -> levelSpawner -> chunk spawner, and assert it in dev. Either way, write it down — this is exactly the class of invented-by-implementer spec the draft elsewhere works hard to avoid.

critiqueProblems.title: Grow progress bar stalls at ~28%: 'top reachable tier' is not derivable from the 4-tier table, and the HUD dash-gauge feed is left unresolved in the draft itself
critiqueProblems.detail: scaleManager.js computes progress exit as TIERS[tierIndex+1].enterTrueRadius when a next tier exists; with T3 'Town' present at enter=6.0m, a tier-2 ball heading to the 1.85m goal shows a bar crawling to (1.85-1.25)/(6.0-1.25) ≈ 13% — the draft says 'tier-2 exit target = GOAL_PROGRESS_RADIUS_M' but the code's only signal for 'last tier' is tierIndex == TIERS.length-1, which T2 is not. Needs an explicit constant. Separately, the hud.js fileChanges entry literally contains the design debate ('gauge01 piggybacked on EVT.GROW payload? NO — ... final: TimeEvent carries gauge01 too') — and the 'final' answer gives the dash gauge a 10Hz update, which makes the gauge-full moment (the dash affordance) feel laggy versus the 0.12s CSS transition.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Add MAX_REACHABLE_TIER = 2 (or a reachable:boolean on the Tier typedef) to the Phase-0 tiers.js contract and key both the progress-exit branch and the dev asserts on it. For the gauge: emit gauge01 on EVT.GROW (already 10Hz) AND on EVT.DASH plus a one-shot 'gauge full' edge event from ballPhysics (it owns the gauge) so the ready-flash is frame-accurate; scrub the debate text out of the spec before freezing.

critiqueProblems.title: Ball reset/start and ?at= teleports don't initialize the y-spring/stand for elevated floors
critiqueProblems.detail: BallPhysics.reset(radiusSim) hardcodes pos=(0, radiusSim, 0) and _ySpring.value=radiusSim — correct for the v1 infinite plane, wrong for v2 where the start is on F1 at h0.45 (stand = 4.5 sim units at boot scale) and ?at=garden|street|plaza teleports land on floors of differing heights and zones with different appropriate radii. Without an extended reset, the ball boots embedded in (or springing up through) the tatami floor, and standSimY tracking (finding 6) starts from a wrong baseline. Teleports also need spawner/levelSpawner/terrain origin state to stay consistent (the v1 dev-teleport rebase key exists, but ?at= jumps are far larger than REBASE_DISTANCE_SIM in one frame).
critiqueProblems.resolution: Extend the Phase-0 contract: BallPhysics.reset(pos, radiusSim, standSimY) (terrain queried by main, not by physics — keeps headless tests dependency-free), seed _ySpring.value = standSimY + radiusSim. Spec ?at= as: set ball pos/radius, force one synchronous spawner band-cache invalidation + preload + levelSpawner.preload, then run one maybeRebase. List the four ?at= points (coords + radius + expected zone) as data in level.js so the dev param can't drift from the authored level.

critiqueProblems.title: Rares R1/R2 are only obtainable in a narrow radius window (0.40 <= r < 0.6/0.7) and the design never says missable rares are intended
critiqueProblems.detail: R1 (candy on P2 TV stand, h0.85 = 0.40 step) and R2 (mug on P3 counter, 0.40 step) require r>=0.40 to climb the furniture, but r<0.6 to still pass the interior doorway (R1, tatami room) and r<0.7 for the front door (R2). With GROWTH_K=10 single-absorb jumps (finding 2), a player can permanently lose 2 of 6 rares without ever seeing them, and the result screen's 'rare x/6' plus RARE_SCORE_BONUS=5000 (vs STATUE_SCORE=10000) makes them a third of the score economy. Possibly intended as replay depth for a 4–8min time attack — but the draft doesn't say so, and the sub-pixel sweep issue (finding 5) currently destroys them outright either way. Note absorb's 3D reach means a large ball can hoover counter-top items WITHOUT climbing (dy is small once ball center exceeds counter height), which partially softens the window — that interaction should be verified, not assumed.
critiqueProblems.resolution: Decide and document: if missable-by-growth is intentional (good for S-rank route depth), state it in the design and surface it ('rares glitter through walls' via the existing sparkle provider so players see what they're skipping). If not, lower P2/P3 steps to 0.30m and rely on door gates alone. Either way add a Stream-A/E test: at r=0.45 and r=0.65, can the ball actually reach each rare's absorb distance given platform footprints (P2 top is 0.9m x 1.4m — a 0.9m-diameter ball barely centers on it)?

terrainDesign: NEW src/world/terrain.js — class Terrain, the only collision/height/occlusion authority. DATA (from src/config/level.js, REAL METERS, static): floors = AA rects {id,x0,z0,x1,z1,h,surf} where h is ABSOLUTE stand height; FLOOR RULE (validator-enforced): any two floor rects are either disjoint or one is contained (boundary-touching allowed) in the other with STRICTLY HIGHER h — contained-higher rects are solid platform prisms; the stand surface at (x,z) is the HIGHEST floor containing the point, else 0 (base ground plane). This makes 'under the table' unrepresentable and stand height single-valued. walls = thin AA segments {id,x0,z0,x1,z1,yTop} (x0===x1 or z0===z1, thickness WALL_THICK_M=0.12, yTop ABSOLUTE). WALLS ARE ALWAYS RIGID: they block at every radius; the climb rule applies ONLY to floor step edges. zones = AA rects {id,bandMask,interior:0|1}. BOOT: bucket floors+walls into a static 4m grid (TERRAIN_BUCKET_M); zero per-frame alloc. UNIT BRIDGE: mirrors Spawner bookkeeping — subscribes EVT.RESCALE (invWorldScale*=S? no: worldScale/=S equivalent — copy spawner's exact handler: scale accumulator *= and origin *= S) and EVT.REBASE (origin += sx,sz); realX=(simX+originX)*worldScale. All queries take CURRENT sim coords, math in real meters, return sim. QUERIES: standSimY(x,z) -> highest-floor stand height in sim units (spawner restY, flight landings, blob shadow, dirLight target); bandMaskAt(x,z) -> zone band bitmask (0 outside all zones = nothing spawns outside the level); interiorAt(x,z) -> 0|1; clampCameraBoom(ballPos,desiredCamPos,outPos) -> segment-vs-wall test in the grid (XZ crossing where segment y at crossing < wall yTopSim), shortens the boom to nearest hit minus CAM_WALL_MARGIN_K*radiusSim, returns whether clamped (cameraRig smooths via its existing critically-damped position spring — no snap); clampToBounds(state) failsafe AABB clamp to LEVEL_BOUNDS. PHYSICS INTEGRATION (ballPhysics.step, per substep, after XZ integration, before y-spring): terrain.collide(state): (a) WALLS: circle(r=radiusSim)-vs-AABB in XZ with rounded segment ends (clean doorway jambs); on contact full de-penetration along XZ normal + reflect normal vel * WALL_RESTITUTION; emits EVT.BOUNCE (PAYLOADS.bounce, 0.25s cooldown) so shake/clonk come free; if ballDiameterReal within DOOR_HINT_FRAC(0.15) of the nearest authored opening width, also emits EVT.DOOR_HINT (HUD nudge). (b) STEP EDGES: candidate = standSimY(newX,newZ); rise = candidateReal - state.standReal; if rise > STEP_CLIMB_K * trueRadius -> per-axis resolve (test (newX,oldZ),(oldX,newZ); revert + zero velocity on offending axes — robust at doorstep corners); descents always allowed. (c) Writes the AUTHORITATIVE BallState.standSimY from the ACCEPTED post-resolution position — never derived from pos.y (kills the spring-transient race). DOWNSTREAM: y-spring target = state.standSimY + radiusSim; when the target DECREASES (drop-off) the spring uses BALL_Y_ZETA_DROP=1.0 (critically damped, no under-floor dip), keeps BALL_Y_ZETA=0.6 for upward absorb pop; spring output hard-clamped to >= standSimY + radiusSim*Y_FLOOR_CLAMP_K(0.98). RESCALE/REBASE: ballPhysics.rescale/rescaleSpring already scale _ySpring; standSimY *= S in rescale and is shift-free in rebase (heights don't shift); terrain origin/scale via bus, handler code copied verbatim from spawner. KNOCK-OFF FLIGHTS (spawner._updateFlights): per flight step recompute f.restY = terrain.standSimY(f.x,f.z) + r*(1+yOff) so landings match the local floor (table-top launches land on the floor below, not in the air); additionally a cheap wall point-test on the moved XZ — on wall contact the flight lands immediately at the contact point (no through-shoji mugs). RENDERING: NEW src/render/terrainMesh.js — ONE merged vertex-colored BufferGeometry built at boot in REAL METERS: each floor rect above its surroundings rendered as a closed box from the surrounding stand height to its h (prism skirts get a darker shade), surf colors per zone (tatami green / wood / genkan stone / garden earth / pond blue / asphalt / kerb grey / plaza tile); walls as closed boxes (shoji white indoors, hedge green, fence grey, plaza stone). MeshLambertMaterial({vertexColors:true}), +1 draw call, inside terrainGroup with group.scale.setScalar(1/worldScale), group.position.set(-originX*?,0,...) — exactly: scale=1/worldScale and position=(-originX,0,-originZ) in sim, updated on EVT.RESCALE/EVT.REBASE; a uniform similarity, pixel-identical at rescale by construction. The v1 infinite ground shader plane remains as the h=0 base. SPAWNER INTEGRATION: constructor gains terrain; in _spawnPlacement AFTER the 'deterministic draws complete' marker (spawner.js:891, dev-assert comment added there): if !((terrain.bandMaskAt(x,z)>>band)&1) return true (deterministic — terrain static); y = terrain.standSimY(x,z) + simR*(1+yOff); same in reinject() (spawner.js:414) and _landObject. COST: ~6-12 bucket-local AABB tests per substep + 1 segment cast for the camera per frame — microseconds.
levelDesign: All coordinates REAL METERS; floor h and wall yTop ABSOLUTE. Ball start (-2.4, 0) on F1, r=0.05m, facing +x. == FLOORS (20, fully enumerated, validator-checked) == F1 tatami/living x[-4.8,0] z[-3.2,3.2] h0.45 | F2 kitchen x[0,4.6] z[0.2,3.2] h0.45 | F3 hallway x[0,4.6] z[-1.2,0.2] h0.45 | F3b washitsu x[0,4.6] z[-3.2,-1.2] h0.45 (fills the v1-draft floor hole; open-plan, no wall to hallway) | F4 genkan x[4.6,5.8] z[-1.2,0.6] h0.25 (0.20 step from hallway) | F4b doorstep stone x[5.8,6.6] z[-1.0,0.4] h0.10 (0.15 step from genkan; 0.10 to garden — re-entry chain needs only r>=0.20) | P1 low table x[-2.9,-1.5] z[1.2,2.6] h0.75 (0.30 step, prism on F1) | P2 TV stand x[-4.8,-3.9] z[-3.2,-1.8] h0.75 (0.30, prism) | P3 kitchen counter x[0.2,2.4] z[2.5,3.2] h0.75 (0.30, prism) | GARDEN (pond carved — no lower-inside-higher overlap): F5a x[5.8,14.5] z[-6.5,3.5] h0 | F5b x[5.8,7.5] z[3.5,6.5] h0 | F5c x[10,14.5] z[3.5,6.5] h0 | F5d x[7.5,10] z[6.0,6.5] h0 | F6 pond x[7.5,10] z[3.5,6.0] h-0.05 (0.05 dip: climbable at every radius >=0.05 — never a trap) | F7 street x[14.5,45] z[-4.5,4.5] h0 | F8 upper street x[30,45] z[-4.5,4.5] h0.35 (prism on F7; kerb step 0.35 at x=30, climb r>=0.35) | FA1 alcove pocket x[24,28] z[4.5,7.5] h0 | FA2 alcove pocket x[33,37] z[-7.5,-4.5] h0 | F9 plaza x[45,72] z[-14,14] h0.35 (flush with F8 through the arch) | F11 statue pedestal x[55.6,58.4] z[-1.4,1.4] h0.85 (prism on F9; 0.50 step). == WALLS (32, all rigid, yTop absolute) == HOUSE (yTop 2.85): W1 x=-4.8 z[-3.2,3.2]; W2 z=3.2 x[-4.8,4.6]; W3 z=-3.2 x[-4.8,4.6]; W4 x=4.6 z[0.6,3.2]; W5 x=4.6 z[-3.2,-1.2]; W6 z=0.6 x[4.6,5.8]; W7 z=-1.2 x[4.6,5.8]; W8 x=5.8 z[0.4,0.6] + W9 x=5.8 z[-1.2,-1.0] (FRONT DOOR opening z[-1.0,0.4] = 1.4m -> pass at r<0.7); W10 x=0 z[-3.2,-1.2] + W11 x=0 z[0.8,3.2] (INTERIOR DOORWAY z[-1.2,0.8] = 2.0m -> r<1.0, wider than the front door so it never binds first). GARDEN HEDGES (yTop 3.0): W12 z=6.5 x[5.8,14.5]; W13 z=-6.5 x[5.8,14.5]; W14 x=14.5 z[1.3,6.5] + W15 x=14.5 z[-6.5,-1.3] (GATE z[-1.3,1.3] = 2.6m -> r<1.3); W16 x=5.8 z[0.6,6.5]; W17 x=5.8 z[-6.5,-1.2] (garden ring CLOSED against house faces — no side-yard dead space; house W/N/S exteriors face void, unreachable, mask 0). STREET FENCES (yTop 1.8, rigid => never climbable, exploit closed): W18 z=4.5 x[14.5,24.6] + W19 z=4.5 x[27.4,45] (A1 opening x[24.6,27.4] = 2.8m); W20 z=-4.5 x[14.5,33.6] + W21 z=-4.5 x[36.4,45] (A2 opening x[33.6,36.4]); A1 pocket: W22 x=24 z[4.5,7.5], W23 x=28 z[4.5,7.5], W24 z=7.5 x[24,28]; A2 pocket: W25 x=33 z[-7.5,-4.5], W26 x=37 z[-7.5,-4.5], W27 z=-7.5 x[33,37]. PLAZA: arch W28 x=45 z[3.5,14] + W29 x=45 z[-14,-3.5] yTop2.5 (ENTRY z[-3.5,3.5] = 7m); perimeter yTop3.0: W30 x=72 z[-14,14]; W31 z=14 x[45,72]; W32 z=-14 x[45,72]. GATE TABLE (monotone outward along the path; validator prints it): interior 2.0m -> front door 1.4m -> garden gate 2.6m -> arch 7m; the front door is the single size gate, protected by the NO-SEAL RULE below. BOUNDS: the wall rings ARE the bounds; failsafe LEVEL_BOUNDS clamp x[-6,73] z[-15,15]. == ZONES (bandMask bit b = band b allowed; interior flag) == Z1 HOUSE x[-4.8,5.8] z[-3.2,3.2] bands{0,1} interior:1 | Z2 GARDEN_NEAR x[5.8,9.5] z[-6.5,6.5] bands{0,1} (band-0 sprinkle so a sub-0.2m escapee can still grow and re-enter) | Z3 GARDEN x[9.5,14.5] z[-6.5,6.5] bands{1,2} | Z4 STREET x[14.5,45] z[-4.5,4.5] bands{1,2} | Z5 ALCOVES (FA1,FA2 rects) bands{2} | Z6 PLAZA x[45,72] z[-14,14] bands{2} | Z7 SCENERY-N x[14.5,45] z[4.5,14] and Z8 SCENERY-S x[14.5,45] z[-14,-4.5] bands{3} ONLY (chunk N+1 scenery — sealed behind fences/arch walls, decorative, NEVER the late meal) — pockets FA1/FA2 override (zone list ordered, first containing zone wins). Outside all zones: mask 0 (chunk spawner silent; backdrop fills the horizon). == CURATED PLACEMENTS (~265 in level.js PLACEMENTS) == HOUSE ~110: coin/die/eraser/paperclip/candy clusters around start; battery+key rows under P1; book rows along W1/W3; mugs+candy on P1/P3 tops; SMALL-VARIANT plushie(sizeMul0.6)/cat(0.7)/trash_can(0.6) ring — NO stool indoors (NO-SEAL: total house absorbable volume tuned so vacuum-everything max radius = 0.67 < 0.70 front-door limit; validator computes and asserts cbrt(0.05^3 + GROWTH_K*SumR3) < 0.95*gateRadius per enclosed region). GARDEN ~45: stools, trash_cans, die-as-stones (gray paletteHex, sizeMul 3), dog, bench by pond, plushies near door (band-0 strip handles tiny stragglers); garden+house budget < 0.95*1.3 (gate 2.6m). STREET ~55: 8 bicycle, 10 person, 5 bench, 4 mailbox, 6 sign, 3 dog, 2 cart, 3 hydrant; alcove pockets dense reward clusters (FA1: bicycles+R4; FA2: carts+signs). UPPER STREET / PLAZA late meal (AUTHORED, not chunk band-3): 5 'car' placements on F8 (x 32-43) + 2 'truck' on the plaza fringe (x 47-52) — car r~0.95 absorbable at ball>=1.47, truck r~1.15 at >=1.77; sized so 1.4 -> 1.8 closes with >=40% surplus (verified ?at=plaza&r=1.4). PLAZA ~50: person crowd thinning toward statue, bench ring, fountain at (62,7) as oversized decoration (sizeMul -> r=2.0, pushback only pre-statue). STATUE: archetypeId 'bronze_statue' (LEVEL_EXTRA, own pool), radiusNominal 1.15, jitter 0, position (57,0) on F11, center y = 0.85+1.15 = 2.00 abs; absorbable by the plain 0.65 rule at trueRadius >= 1.77m; teased: visible down the straight street once fogFar >= ~28m (r>=0.51 with FOG_FAR_MIN, comfortably by mid-street) and framed by the arch. Post-absorb size = cbrt(1.77^3+10*1.15^3) ~ 2.75m (result-screen copy and bounds account for this; input is zeroed in CELEBRATE so rigid 3.0m rings hold regardless). == RARES (6, rare:true, paletteHex 0xffd24a + sparkle; missable-by-growth is INTENTIONAL replay depth, documented on the result screen as レア x/6) == R1 candy on P2 top (-4.3,-2.5) window r[0.30,0.67] | R2 mug on P3 top (1.2,2.9) window r[0.30,0.67] | R3 plushie 'golden frog' in pond (8.0,4.0) | R4 bicycle in A1 (26,6.6) | R5 dog plaza corner (48,-12.5) | R6 hydrant behind fountain (63.2,8.4). == DEV_STARTS (data in level.js, consumed by ?at=) == house:(-2.4,0,r0.05) garden:(8,0,r0.45) street:(20,0,r0.80) plaza:(47,0,r1.40). == VALIDATOR (level.js export validateLevel(), dev builds, runs at boot) == asserts: (1) floor pairs disjoint-or-contained-higher; (2) every wall-enclosed region floor-covered (no h=0 pits indoors); (3) reachability of every floor region from start for some r in [0.05,2.2] under step/gate rules; (4) gate table printed; (5) NO-SEAL: per region, max-achievable radius < 0.95 * narrowest-exit radius; (6) all placements inside a floor and inside their archetype's band-sane zone; (7) rares reachable at their window radii. == BACKDROP == NEW src/render/backdrop.js: camera-following low-poly mountain silhouette ring (2 depth layers, vertex colors pre-lerped toward fog color) + big sun disc at LIGHT_DIR azimuth; MeshBasicMaterial vertexColors, fog:false, depthWrite:false, renderOrder -9, radius 0.92*SKY_RADIUS; never in world space => rescale/rebase-proof.
